data.title: Crinetics Pharma: Strong Progress With Multiple Indications And Upcoming PDUFA
data.description:
I covered Crinetics Pharma (NASDAQ:CRNX) back in 2023, when it was developing Paltusotine for acromegaly in a phase 3 trial. Now, they have positive data and a September 25 PDUFA date, so let us take a look.

Background

Lead asset Paltusotine is an oral, selective, non-peptide, somatostatin receptor type 2 (SST2) agonist. An alternative to injectable broad spectrum somatostatin analogs, paltusotine is a non-peptide small molecule that only targets SST2, one of the five somatostatin receptor subtypes. This makes it different from traditional somatostatin analogs (e.g., octreotide, lanreotide), which are peptide-based biologies. Activating SST2 receptors may lead to effects such as inhibition of hormone secretion (e.g., growth hormone, insulin, glucagon) and tumor growth suppression in neuroendocrine tumors (NETs). As I noted before:

In acromegaly, currently available peptide drugs need painful monthly or daily injections, or a strict twice-a-day oral dosing regimen. Current somatostatin peptide drugs often fail to halt disease progression.

Current standard of care in acromegaly is resection, however, surgery alone does not always help normalize GH and IGF levels. Radiation and adjuvant pharmacotherapy - somatostatin analogues, dopamine agonists and growth hormone antagonists - are needed to normalize those hormones.

Paltusotine is an oral SRL (Somatostatin Receptor Ligands) like Mycapssa, however, and the company claims its small molecule nature gives it certain advantages. Mycapssa is oral octreotide, and while non-inferiority trials have been positive, in practice, oral octreotide is generally considered of superior efficacy if more difficult to administer. In a phase 2 study called ACROBAT, which had both Paltusotine and other oral SRLs, data were confusing because of the non-randomized nature of the trial, and then generally loose trial design. So, an OLE study was performed, which “saw that paltusotine lowered and maintained IGF-1 at levels comparable to prior injected SRL therapy for up to 103 weeks, and that at Week 52, thirty-two (88.9%) of the 36 respondents preferred once daily paltusotine treatment.” However, we still needed a thorough and well-designed trial.

The two phase 3 PATHFINDER studies tested paltusotine’s ability to reduce IGF levels in two patient populations - one octreotide-treated group, and the other treatment-naïve.

Data

Data from the PATHFINDER-1 study was announced in September 2023, and from the PATHFINDER-2 study in March 2024. In both studies, paltusotine met the primary and all secondary endpoints. Below, I put the data side by side in a single table.

What we see here is that, unsurprisingly, pretreated patients did better, numerically, than treatment naive patients. We can see that they had a higher IGF-1 control rate (83% vs. 56%), suggesting that those who were already stable on injectables transitioned better to paltusotine. They had better GH suppression (87% vs. 57%), demonstrating that moving from injectables preserved hormonal control better. Interestingly, the untreated group had a larger IGF-1 reduction, but we have to understand that this was simply because they started at a higher baseline IGF-1, rather than showing better efficacy. Pretreated patients started in control and remained stable, therefore their IGF-1 reduction range seems low, but is not actually so.

If you compare this data with the mycapssa Optimal trial where the defined IGF-1 levels range was similar to paltusotine, we see that the former saw ~60% of patients maintained IGF-I response vs ~20% of patients on placebo; while paltusotine had 83% of participants maintained IGF-1 levels compared to 4% on placebo. Placebo-adjusted data for paltusotine was much superior.

Jefferies analysts see a $300mn opportunity in acromegaly for the company.

Other Updates

Last year, paltusotine saw a win in a second indication, carcinoid syndrome, where a phase 2 trial demonstrated “rapid and sustained reductions in flushing episodes and bowel movement.” Key data:

  • 63% reduction in mean flushing frequency for patients with >1/day at baseline (n=24; p<0.0001)
  • 60% reduction in mean excess BM frequency (defined as daily bowel movements above the upper limit of normal, 3/day) in patients with >3/day at baseline (n=16; p=0.02)
  • 61% reduction in mean flushing severity (n=31; p<0.0001) and 64% reduction in mean BM urgency (n=31; p<0.0001)
  • Reductions in frequency and severity of symptoms were observed within 2 weeks of paltusotine treatment and sustained through 8 weeks in both naïve/untreated patients and those switching from prior somatostatin receptor ligand (SRL') therapy

Around 12,000 of Americans are diagnosed with neuroendocrine tumors every year. 20% of these, or roughly 2400 patients, have carcinoid syndrome. Injectable SRLs are standardly used for the disease, however, like with acromegaly, they have strong side effects.

A second phase 2 trial for atumelnant in congenital adrenal hyperplasia (CAH') also reported positive data in January, although the stock traded “sharply lower” on the data. Data saw the molecule produce ““rapid, substantial, and sustained” reductions in A4 levels with a statistically significant effect across all atumelnant doses after 12 weeks of therapy.” The company plans a global phase 3 trial in the indication, a genetic disease characterized by excessive A4 levels.

According to a source:

Classic CAH affects as many as 1 in every 10,000 to 15,000 people living in the United States and Europe. Nonclassic CAH affects about 1 in every 100 to 200 people. Both classic and nonclassic CAH affect people worldwide.

Apart from hormone replacement and symptom control using glucocorticoids, Crenessity from Neurocrine (NBIX), approved in December, is the key rival. In trials, data was as follows:

The group that received Crenessity reduced their daily glucocorticoid dose by 27% while maintaining control of androstenedione levels, compared to a 10% daily glucocorticoid dose reduction in the group that received placebo.

Also:

At week 4, androstenedione levels decreased with crinecerfont (-299 ng per deciliter) but increased with placebo (45.5 ng per deciliter) (least-squares mean difference, -345 ng per deciliter; P<0.001).

CRNX data is from 12 weeks:

While baseline A4 levels were different, Atumelnant achieved much higher reductions.

Financials

CRNX has a market cap of $3.12bn and a cash balance of $1.4bn. Research and development expenses were $66.6 million for the three months ended December 31, 2024, while general and administrative expenses were $28.2 million. Their CFO for the quarter was ($64.7mn) while capex was ($1.0mn), which gives them an FCF of -$65.7 and a cash runway of 21 quarters.

Risks

The stock has a sizable 8.35% short interest, which shows not everyone is seeing the rosy picture here. It is also confusing to see the stock going down on releasing positive data for Atumelnant in CAH, possibly due to Crenessity having been approved as a rival just the previous month. Other than that, there’s not a lot to complain about with CRNX, except perhaps the fact that its lead indication targets a smallish market.

Bottom Line

CRNX has a PDUFA in 6 months and the stock is trading quite down right now, presenting an opportunity before the PDUFA. However, for longer-term investors, more clarity on the CAH situation is needed, which happens to be the larger market.

data.title: Shiseido: Struggling To Regain Momentum
data.description:
Shiseido (OTCPK:SSDOY) has experienced a continuous decline in its profitability in its latest fiscal year. I believe that this company is not currently well-positioned for a sustainable recovery in the future because its sales and demand in China, which has become an increasingly important geographic location for the company's revenue, has been experiencing weakness. Additionally, the company's SG&A margin is significantly higher than that of its competitors, thereby impacting its profitability and pricing competitiveness. Lastly, I compiled the competitive metrics of Shiseido and its competitors and found that its lack of competitive edge has been contributing to its decline in popularity among brands.

Based on the chart, Japan is the largest region where Shiseido’s revenue comes from. However, the share of revenue from Japan has declined significantly from 38.09% in 2019 to only 22.26% in 2022, before recovering to 28.65% in 2024.

Meanwhile, China, which accounted for the second largest revenue share and only slightly trailing behind Japan, has increased its contribution from 19.11% to 25.23% in the same period. Notably, China was once the largest segment of the company back in 2021 and 2022, when Japan was the only region that was experiencing a decline in its revenue. The revenue shares from all other regions remain relatively stable during this period.

In terms of revenue, Japan experienced the largest historical annual average decline of 6.8%. The revenue from this region decreased sharply especially during the COVID-19 years before recovering starting from 2023. On the other hand, revenue from China remained relatively strong throughout the pandemic due to the increase in duty free allowance in China downtown implemented during this period. However, the revenue from China has been experiencing negative growth since 2022 and only a slight 0.8% increase in 2024.

I believe the announcement of the discharge of Fukushima wastewater, which has led to increased tension and boycott of Chinese consumers toward Japanese products, have impacted on the sales of the company from China. Also, according to management, the change in spending pattern of these Chinese consumers has also impacted its Travel Retail Business, which has faced flattish historical average growth of only 0.1%, driven by the sharp declines seen starting in 2023.

The EMEA, which is the third largest region of the company, has seen the highest growth of 3.6% among all other regions. I believe this growth is driven by Shiseido’s focus on expanding physical stores for its UK business, which is evidenced by its retail sales growing by around 20% in 2023.

Also, the company has been addressing the under penetration of fragrance business in its UK market, which only represented 20% of its revenue in the UK. Shiseido has recently signed a long-term fragrance partnership with Max Mara, a European company, which I believe could expand Shiseido’s portfolio in the fragrance market, especially within the EMEA region.

Overall, Japan remains as the region with the largest revenue share of the company, though I believe that China’s significance in the company has been increasing, as evidenced by its share of revenue follows closely to that of the Japan market and has once surpassed Japan to be the largest region of the company revenue. However, the revenue from the China market has been showing weaknesses, which could impact the company’s revenue growth trajectory given its increasing importance.

Profitability Comparison

Next, the performance metrics of Shiseido and its competitors is compiled to identify the trends in their growth and determine if Shiseido’s weak performance is due to overall market weakness or company specific factors.

Image created by author with data from Annual Reports

Shiseido's main competitors include L'Oreal (OTCPK:LRLCY), Estee Lauder (EL), and Coty (COTY). According to the chart, Shiseido’s performance across all metrics is the weakest among competitors except for its gross margin. While the overall market revenue grew by an annual average of 5.8% in the past 5 years, Shiseido’s revenue only grew by 2.1%, significantly underperforming its competitors. Though it has the second highest gross margin of 76%, its operating margin (0.8%) and net margin (-1.1%) are much lower than the competitor’s average of 9% and 4.6% respectively.

Though management attributed the decline in operating and net margins to the non-recurring expenses related to structural reform aimed at regaining growth in Japan, after adding back this expenses of 28.8 billion yen, an operating margin of 3.7% and net margin of 1.8% are still derived, which are still well below that of the competitors.

I also noted that Shiseido has the highest SG&A margin of 75.9%, which is higher than the competitor’s average of 62.1%. I believe this substantially higher SG&A expenses, relative to revenue, contributes to the underperformance of operating profit margin and net profit margin of the company.

In view of this higher-than-peers SG&A margin, management has since 2022 implemented a business strategy called “SHIFT 2025 and Beyond” which includes several structural reform plans and targeted a 15% core operating profit by 2027.

However, I compared the recent years’ performance of the company with the targeted margin and noted that there is a significant shortfall. For instance, the core operating margin, which is expected to increase to 9% in 2024, has fallen from 4.1% from 2023 to 3.7% in 2024 in contrast.

Meanwhile, SG&A expenses excluding non-recurring items, have also continued to increase from 71.2% in 2023 to 73% in 2024. I suppose this indicates that the company hasn’t been demonstrating its effectiveness in addressing the high SG&A margin concern, thereby impacting its profitability margins. Also, I believe that the company’s target for its profitability is unlikely to be achieved by 2027, therefore I think taking a more conservative approach for valuation might be more appropriate.

Competitive Analysis

Lastly, the competitive metrics of Shiseido with its peers is analyzed to determine if Shiseido has any competitive edge that will help it to outperform the market and improve its margins moving forward.

The breadth of the brands portfolio of a company could indicate its ability to cater to the different consumer segments and preferences, while having a stronger geographic presence could reflect the extent to which a company has access to the markets and consumer bases. Meanwhile, having a higher R&D margin may show a company’s commitment to product innovation to address consumer needs, and the number of patents protects a company’s intellectual property resulted the R&D. The Net Promoter Score tracks if the consumer of a brand “would recommend using the product based on a scale of -100 to 100”. A higher score would mean that consumers are more satisfied with the brand, therefore creating more value for the company through popularity among consumers.

According to the ranking, L’Oreal holds the top position by having the greatest number of brands under its portfolio, strongest global geographic presence, and the highest number of patents. This top ranking is also supported by L’Oreal, being the company with the highest profitability and highest revenue growth, as shown in the last section. Meanwhile, Shiseido ranks third among its peers. Notably, it has a high R&D margin, which I suppose resulted in its relatively high number of patents. However, despite having the highest R&D margin, its product portfolio is still less extensive than that of L’Oreal and Coty, which would limit its addressable market as compared to its competitors.

I look into the Google search trends over the past 12 months to determine the popularity of each brand among customers worldwide.

Based on the data provided by Google Trends, the popularity of the Shiseido search term is significantly lower than that of the other three competitors on average. I believe this indicates that the consumer awareness and interest globally for Shiseido may not be as prominent as that of its competitors. This lower visibility might diminish the brand effect of Shiseido, especially in terms of online presence.

Risk: Shift in Consumer Preference in China

The China market accounts for the second largest revenue in the company. In recent years, there has been a shift in consumer preference for buying Japanese goods due to increased diplomatic tension between Japan and China, with the Fukushima waste water incident further deteriorating the trade relationship. Additionally, as noted by management in the latest earnings call, Shiseido’s pricing in the mass market is high relative to the Chinese competitors. Specifically for consumer products brands such as AUPRES, management stated that they are not able to offer “competitive pricing to compete with China’s local brands” for now. I believe this would mean that the Chinese consumers might prefer to purchase cheaper products from the local brands competitors, and if Shiseido's profitability margins may be impacted if it were to lower its prices to maintain its market share in China.

Verdict

Overall, I believe that revenue from China is increasingly more significant to the company’s performance, yet the revenue from this market has been declining due to more intense competition from Chinese local brands as well as the wastewater effect on Chinese consumers’ spending behavior. Also, Shiseido’s SG&A is much higher than that of its competitors and has not been showing any notable improvement since its announcement of strategic reform. Therefore, I believe its peers maintain a competitive edge over Shiseido on cost structure. Moreover, Shiseido only ranks third when its competitive metrics are compared against its competitors. Its popularity among consumers is also the lowest among all three brands, which I believe suggests the lower visibility and interest in Shiseido’s products.

The revenue of each region is projected based on their 3-year average revenue growth and tapered off the growth of 0.5% each year starting from 2026 as I would like to remain conservative about the company’s performance, given that the company does not possess a strong competitive edge in its product offerings.

Image created by author with data from SeekingAlpha

Based on the 5-yr Industry Average EV/EBITDA (19.96x) of 9 cosmetics companies- Estee Lauder (EL), L'Oreal (OTCPK:LRLCY), Coty (COTY), Shiseido (OTCPK:SSDOY), Kose (OTCPK:KOSCF), LVMH (OTCPK:LVMHF), Procter & Gamble (PG), L'Occitane (OTCPK:LCCTF), and Unilever (UL), and a WACC of 5.4%, a price target of $15.35 is derived, which represents a downside of 18.8%. Hence, I rate the company as a Sell.

Conclusion

Overall, I believe Shiseido’s revenue from its main markets, Japan and China, have been underperforming due to the company’s lack of competitiveness in terms of its product offerings and consumer awareness. On top of the below average revenue growth in the past 5 years, the profitability of the company is also underperforming its peers due to high SG&A and the company’s ineffectiveness in implementing its restructuring plan, which has failed to meet its targeted profitability metrics. I believe this disadvantage in cost structure further limits the company’s competitiveness in the China market, where the local brands are able to offer products to mass market at a comparatively lower price.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Enigmatig Pursues IPO On Deteriorating Fundamentals
data.description:
Enigmatig’s Revenue Dropping Along With Operating Results

Enigmatig Ltd (EGG) has filed to raise $16.9 million in an IPO of its Class A ordinary shares, according to an SEC F-1 registration statement.

EGG provides licensing services and related regulatory maintenance services to clients internationally.

Topline revenue growth is falling, and financial results are declining.

Given the firm’s declining financial results, exuberant valuation assumptions and numerous risks, my outlook on the EGG IPO is to sell (avoid it).

What Does Enigmatig Do?

Singapore-based Enigmatig Limited was founded to develop a range of corporate licensing and regulatory compliance services primarily aimed at small- and medium-sized businesses in the greater Asia-Pacific region.

Management is headed by founder, Chairman and CEO Mr. Foo Chee Weng Desmond, who has been with the firm since its inception in 2019 and was previously senior associate at Saxo Capital Markets Pte and a co-founder at Blackwell Global Investments.

The company’s primary offerings include the following:

  • Business licenses

  • Brokerage licenses

  • Regulatory consultancy services

  • Corporate secretarial services

As of Sept. 30, 2024, Enigmatig has booked fair market value investment of $117,370 from investors, including the founder and various individuals.

The company pursues clients from among small- and medium-sized companies seeking business, brokerage or other regulatory licensing or compliance services.

In fiscal 2024, the firm counted 55 clients from 15 different jurisdictions.

However, it has significant customer concentration risk, with its two largest customers accounting for 47.9% of its corporate services revenue in the most recent fiscal year.

Payroll & Employee Benefits costs as a function of topline revenue have fallen as revenues have decreased, as the figures below indicate:

Payroll & Employee Benefits

Expenses vs. Revenue

(Source - SEC)

The Payroll & Employee Benefits efficiency multiple, i.e., how much new revenue is generated by each dollar of Payroll & Employee Benefits expense, was negative (1.5x) in the most recent reporting period, a poor result. (Source - SEC)

The firm has little recent activity regarding related party transactions, although previous activity was partially settled via digital asset transfers.

Market Overview For Enigmatig

According to a 2024 market research report by Grand View Research, the Asia-Pacific market for regulatory consulting outsourcing services was an estimated $343.7 million in 2023 and is expected to exceed $676 million by 2030.

This represents a forecast CAGR of 10.2% from 2024 to 2030.

The primary reasons for this expected growth are increasingly complex regulatory compliance requirements, changing supply chain dynamics, and the need for greater automation of licensing and compliance efforts to increase efficiencies.

The company faces an increasingly fragmented and competitive market environment. Its competitors not only include direct competition from other consulting-type companies, but also from law firms and accountancy companies that provide many of the same services as they seek to increase their client "share of wallet."

Also, the chart shown here illustrated the historical and expected growth trajectory for the industry through 2030:

I’ve prepared a thumbnail SWOT analysis for Enigmatig:

Strengths

  • One-stop service offerings, local presence in major hubs

Weaknesses

  • Customer concentration, limited service diversification

Opportunities

  • Geographic expansion, M&A possibilities, technology investment

Threats

  • Fragmented competition, geopolitical risks, regulatory complexity

Enigmatig’s Recent Financial Results

The firm’s recent financial performance is summarized as shown below:

  • Declining topline revenue from a small base

  • Reduced gross profit and gross margin

  • Lower operating profit

  • Increasing cash used in operations

As of Sept. 30, 2024, Enigmatig had $1.6 million in cash and $1.7 million in total liabilities.

The company has generated free cash flow for the twelve months ended September 30, 2024, of negative ($606,109).

IPO Details For Enigmatig

Enigmatig intends to raise $16.9 million in gross proceeds from an IPO of its Class A ordinary shares, offering 3.75 million shares at a proposed midpoint price of $4.50 each.

No existing shareholders have indicated an interest in purchasing additional shares at the IPO price.

With a completed IPO, the firm’s enterprise value would be approximately $115 million, not including the effects of underwriter over-allotment options.

The float to outstanding shares ratio (ex-underwriter over-allotments) will be approximately 13.04%.

The company, executives and directors and major shareholders have agreed to a standard 180-day shareholder sale or issuance lock-up period.

Management disclosed it intends to apply the net proceeds from the IPO as follows:

approximately 30%, [...] is expected to be used for research and development for software;

approximately 40%, [...] is expected to be used for expansion through strategic acquisitions and investments. As of the date of this prospectus, no acquisition target has been identified;

approximately 25%, [...] is expected to be used for increasing sales and marketing capabilities; and

the balance of the net proceeds for other working capital and general corporate purposes.

(Source - SEC)

The company's online roadshow is not available.

Regarding outstanding legal proceedings, the leadership said the company is not presently a party to any administrative or legal proceedings that are likely to have a material adverse effect on its operations or financial condition.

The sole listed bookrunner of the IPO is Prime Number Capital.
